subject: Fixing docker image pull rate issue
commit_message:
Fixing docker image pull rate issue

Pull applicable images from Nexus (proxy) instead of dockeer hub.

Issue-ID: CCSDK-2989

Signed-off-by: BjornMagnussonXA <bjorn.magnusson@est.tech>
Change-Id: Iab635867393cbc0c08605d4b93149d2609da9374

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/ccsdk-oran-master-verify-csit-polmansuite/15/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/ccsdk-oran-master-verify-csit-polmansuite/15

Patch Set 1:

would it really fix the issue or move it
you believe that the nexus will trustable from docker hub?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> would it really fix the issue or move it
> you believe that the nexus will trustable from docker hub?

Yes, I think it will fix the problem. According to Nexus documentation, my understanding is that Nexus will act as a proxy and cache for images from docker hub. So the image will be pulled from docker hub only for the first pull attempt towards Nexus for a specific image tag. Once the image exists in Nexus, the image will be pulled only from Nexus.
Further reading here: https://help.sonatype.com/repomanager3/repository-management
However, I dont know if this is the long term solution. Perhaps a local image repository only for CSIT could be considered.  

There are other projects doing the same thing, e.g. : https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/integration/csit/+/114847

OK thanks for the explanation

I will ask LF IT about it
because as far as I understood we refused to host third party dockers for legal issues..if we cache them, it is not very different from hosting them..
Change has been successfully merged by Morgan Richomme
